title: Kibatalia macrophylla
taxonomy: Kibatalia macrophylla is a species within the family Sapotaceae. This family belongs to the order Ebenales. Further classification places the Sapotaceae family within the clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The order Ebenales and the clade angiosperms fall within the larger group of flowering plants known as magnoliophytes. The classification of Kibatalia macrophylla places it within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Kibatalia macrophylla, a plant native to the Philippines, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant's leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and bark of Kibatalia macrophylla are also used to make tea, which is believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Kibatalia macrophylla.
